claims.evidence_text: RUE_total ... ranged from 1.86 (24th ESWYT) to 2.16 (37th ESWYT) g MJ -1 with a respective annual progress rate of 0.91%
claims.claim_summary: Total radiation use efficiency of CIMMYT elite spring wheat ranged 1.86-2.16 g MJ-1.

claims.evidence_text: Overall BM ranged from 1.45 (24th ESWYT) to 1.78 (37th ESWYT) kg m -2
claims.claim_summary: Wheat biomass at maturity ranged 1.45-1.78 kg/m2 in CIMMYT elite spring wheat trials.

claims.evidence_text: grain filling rate (GFR), biomass at crop maturity (BM) ... grain weight per Spike (GWP) exhibited stronger and more significant positive correlations with GY
claims.claim_summary: Grain filling rate and grain filling period are key drivers of wheat grain yield genetic gains.

claims.evidence_text: At sowing, ∼100 kg ha-1 N (granulated urea) ... was followed by another N application around the first node initiation of 120 kg ha-1
claims.claim_summary: Optimally-irrigated wheat received ~100 kg/ha N at sowing plus 120 kg/ha N at first node (~220 total).

claims.evidence_text: At sowing, ∼100 kg ha-1 N (granulated urea) ... which was followed by another N application around the first node initiation of 120 kg ha-1 (granulated urea).
claims.claim_summary: Wheat N split: at sowing and again at first node initiation.

claims.evidence_text: all trials were arranged in an alpha lattice design with three replicates and a 6.1 m 2 plot size with a seed rate of 120 kg ha -1
claims.claim_summary: CIMMYT elite spring wheat yield trials used a seed rate of 120 kg/ha.

claims.evidence_text: genotypes were grown ... with about 500 mm of available water ... five irrigations of approximately 100 mm each and equally distributed across the growing season
claims.claim_summary: Optimally irrigated wheat at Obregon received ~500 mm water (five 100-mm irrigations).
